title: Tapping for Inherited Religion
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for unpacking what you were handed and what you want to keep
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the complicated feeling of having been given a religion in childhood that no longer fits in adult life. Use it when the inherited framework sits uncomfortably and you are working out what, if anything, to keep.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the religion you grew up with is creating friction with who you are now, and you have not yet worked out what you believe in its place.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the inherited religious framework that sits badly in adult life. Use it when you are carrying someone else's belief system and have not yet sorted through it to find your own.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I was handed a set of beliefs as a child and I don't know which ones still fit, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though questioning my inherited religion feels like a betrayal of the people who gave it to me, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I don't have a replacement framework and the not-knowing is uncomfortable, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** I was given these beliefs before I could choose them.
**Side of eye:** Some of them still fit. Some do not.
**Under eye:** It feels disloyal to sort through them.
**Under nose:** And disloyal to myself to keep the ones that don't fit.
**Chin:** I don't have to decide everything today.
**Collarbone:** The not-knowing is allowed to take as long as it takes.
**Under arm:** I can hold this lightly while I figure it out.
**Top of head:** I am allowed to think for myself about what I believe.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** I can sort through what I was given.
**Side of eye:** Keeping what still fits is allowed.
**Under eye:** Letting go of what doesn't fit is also allowed.
**Under nose:** This is not disloyalty. It is my own faith being built.
**Chin:** The people who handed me these beliefs also changed over their lives.
**Collarbone:** I am open to knowing what I believe.
**Under arm:** My spiritual life belongs to me.
**Top of head:** I can take this slowly and get it right.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy.
